name: Water 7
description: Water 7 is a metropolis built on water, where canals replace streets and boats replace cars. Known as the world's premier shipbuilding city, it's home to the legendary Galley-La Company and some of the greatest shipwrights alive. The city is built on multiple levels connected by waterways, with the wealthy living higher up and the poor closer to sea level. Every year, the city is threatened by Aqua Laguna, a massive tidal wave that forces evacuations. Water 7 is where the Straw Hats faced their greatest internal crisis - learning the Going Merry could not be repaired, Usopp leaving the crew, Robin's mysterious disappearance, and the devastating revelation that their friends at Galley-La were actually CP9 assassins. It's a city of beauty hiding dark secrets, where the World Government's shadow looms large.
history: Water 7's golden age was during Tom's era, when the legendary fish-man shipwright created both Gold Roger's Oro Jackson and the Sea Train that saved the city's economy. However, Tom was framed by Spandam (then CP5) using stolen blueprints to build Battle Frankys that attacked the city. Tom was executed at Enies Lobby, but not before passing the Pluton blueprints to his apprentice Iceburg, who secretly gave them to Franky. Years later, CP9 infiltrated Water 7 for five years, with agents posing as shipwrights at Galley-La to get close to Iceburg and discover the blueprints' location. When the Straw Hats arrived, their ship was deemed unrepairable, causing Usopp to challenge Luffy and leave the crew. Robin disappeared after being blackmailed by CP9 - they threatened to invoke a Buster Call on the Straw Hats if she didn't cooperate. CP9's assassination attempt on Iceburg failed, their identities were exposed, and they captured Robin and Franky. The Straw Hats and Franky Family united to pursue them, leading to the assault on Enies Lobby. After the battle, Franky used the money stolen from the Straw Hats to build the Thousand Sunny from Adam Wood, and joined the crew as their shipwright.
culture: Water 7's culture revolves around shipbuilding mastery and respect for the craft. The city values skill, with Galley-La Company employing only the best. Citizens use Bulls (swimming creatures) or small boats to navigate the canals. The Aqua Laguna shapes the city's calendar and architecture - everyone knows when to evacuate to higher ground. There's a divide between the legitimate Galley-La workers and the Franky Family dismantlers who salvage ships. Despite this, both groups share pride in their city's shipbuilding legacy. Tom's motto 'Do it with a DON!' still resonates throughout the shipwright community.
transportation: Bulls (swimming creatures similar to horses) pull water taxis through canals. Small boats and gondolas are everywhere. The Sea Train connects to other islands. Yagara Bulls are the most common - peaceful creatures that navigate the waterways. Rope-ways and water elevators move between city levels. The Shift Station regulates Bull traffic.

quirks: Water Water Meat as local specialty

notablePeople.name: Iceburg
notablePeople.description: Tom's apprentice and one of the world's greatest shipwrights. Secretly held the Pluton blueprints before giving them to Franky. Target of CP9's assassination. A sophisticated leader who cares deeply for his city.

notablePeople.name: Franky (Cutty Flam)
notablePeople.description: Tom's other apprentice who rebuilt himself as a cyborg after being hit by the Sea Train. Holds the Pluton blueprints and burns them to show faith in Robin. Cola-powered with a flamboyant personality. Becomes the Straw Hats' shipwright.

notablePeople.name: Rob Lucci
notablePeople.description: The strongest CP9 member with the Cat-Cat Fruit Model: Leopard. Posed as a quiet shipwright for 5 years to get close to Iceburg. Ruthless assassin who believes in 'Dark Justice' - killing anyone in the way of the mission.

notablePeople.name: Kaku
notablePeople.description: A CP9 agent with a square nose who posed as Galley-La's Dock 1 foreman. Ate the Ox-Ox Fruit Model: Giraffe during the arc. Actually became friends with the shipwrights, showing some conflict about his mission.

notablePeople.name: Kalifa
notablePeople.description: A CP9 agent with the Bubble-Bubble Fruit who posed as Iceburg's secretary. Her position gave CP9 close access to their target. Uses 'Sexual Harassment!' as a catchphrase.

notablePeople.name: Blueno
notablePeople.description: A CP9 agent with the Door-Door Fruit, allowing him to create doors anywhere, including in the air. Ran a bar as his cover. His Devil Fruit makes him the perfect infiltrator and escape artist.

notablePeople.name: Paulie
notablePeople.description: A rope-using shipwright and Dock 1 foreman. Gambling addict and woman-hater (finds revealing clothes offensive). Loyal to Iceburg and fought against CP9 when their identities were revealed.

notablePeople.name: Kokoro
notablePeople.description: A mermaid disguised as a human who was Tom's secretary. Now runs Shift Station and helps the Straw Hats understand the city and Tom's history. Heavy drinker.

notablePeople.name: Chimney
notablePeople.description: An energetic young girl who is 1/4 mermaid. Always with her pet Gonbe. Provides crucial information and helps guide the rescue efforts.

notablePeople.name: Zambai
notablePeople.description: One of Franky's top subordinates who leads operations when Franky is absent. Square-headed with a strong sense of loyalty to his boss.

notablePeople.name: Monkey D. Luffy
notablePeople.description: Faced his toughest leadership challenge - fighting his own crewmate Usopp over the Merry's fate, then losing Robin. His determination to save Robin no matter what defined his resolve as captain.

notablePeople.name: Usopp
notablePeople.description: Challenged Luffy's decision to abandon the Merry and leave the crew after losing the duel. His attachment to the ship represented his own insecurities about being useful.

notablePeople.name: Nico Robin
notablePeople.description: Sacrificed herself to save the crew from a Buster Call, believing she brings only misfortune. Her departure and the reason behind it became the emotional core of the arc.

notablePeople.name: Spandam
notablePeople.description: The incompetent, sadistic leader of CP9 who framed Tom years ago. Obsessed with finding Pluton to gain power and recognition. Weak fighter but dangerous due to his authority and Buster Call authorization.

subLocations.name: Galley-La Company Headquarters
subLocations.description: The massive headquarters of the world's premier shipbuilding company. Multiple docks with different specialties. Iceburg's office is at the top. The site of CP9's assassination attempt.

subLocations.name: Dock 1
subLocations.description: The main shipbuilding dock where the Going Merry was examined. Where the devastating news that she couldn't be repaired was delivered. Managed by foremen including Paulie and Kaku.

subLocations.name: Water 7 Canals
subLocations.description: The intricate canal system that serves as the city's streets. Multiple levels with bridges and water elevators. Bulls pull boats through the flowing waterways. Beautiful but dangerous during Aqua Laguna.

subLocations.name: Franky House
subLocations.description: The Franky Family's base of operations where they dismantle ships and store salvaged parts. Located in the backstreets. Where Usopp was beaten and the money stolen, triggering the crew's crisis.

subLocations.name: Blueno's Bar
subLocations.description: A seemingly ordinary bar run by Blueno as his cover. Actually a CP9 meeting place where they could discuss their mission in secret using Blueno's Door-Door Fruit to create hidden rooms.

subLocations.name: Shift Station
subLocations.description: The Sea Train station where Kokoro works as stationmaster. The departure point for the Puffing Tom and later Rocket Man. Central hub for information about Tom's legacy.

subLocations.name: Iceburg's Mansion
subLocations.description: The mayor's elegant mansion where the CP9 assassination attempt occurred. Multiple floors with Iceburg's private quarters at the top. The scene of betrayal as trusted allies revealed themselves as assassins.

subLocations.name: Scrap Island
subLocations.description: An island of discarded ships and parts where Franky stores his projects. Where the Luffy vs Usopp duel took place - a devastating fight between captain and crewmate.

items.name: Pluton Blueprints
items.description: The complete blueprints for the ancient weapon Pluton, passed down through generations of Tom's apprentices. Franky burns them to show faith in Robin, trusting she won't revive the weapon.

items.name: Adam Wood
items.description: The strongest and rarest wood in the world, used to build Gold Roger's ship and later the Thousand Sunny. Extremely expensive and difficult to obtain.

items.name: Going Merry's Keel
items.description: The damaged keel that made the Merry unrepairable. The breaking point that triggered Usopp's departure and the crew's greatest crisis.

items.name: Golden Den Den Mushi
items.description: Spandam's special transponder snail that can authorize a Buster Call - a fleet of Marine battleships that annihilates entire islands. The threat that made Robin surrender.

items.name: Buster Call Authorization
items.description: The authority to summon ten battleships and five Vice Admirals to obliterate a target. CP9 used this threat to force Robin's cooperation.

items.name: CP9 Keys
items.description: Keys held by CP9 members. One unlocks Robin's seastone handcuffs, but nobody knows which one.

items.name: 200 Million Berries
items.description: The money stolen from Usopp by the Franky Family, intended for ship repairs. Later used by Franky to buy Adam Wood for the Thousand Sunny.

items.name: Aqua Laguna
items.description: The massive annual tidal wave that threatens Water 7. The largest in years occurred during this arc, flooding the lower levels and creating chaos.

factions.name: Galley-La Company
factions.description: The world's premier shipbuilding company, infiltrated by CP9 for five years. The betrayal devastated the legitimate workers.

factions.name: CP9
factions.description: Elite World Government assassins who infiltrated Water 7 for five years to obtain the Pluton blueprints. Their identities' reveal shocked the city.

factions.name: Franky Family
factions.description: Ship dismantlers who operate in Water 7's backstreets. Initially antagonistic to the Straw Hats but become allies when the truth about CP9 emerges.

factions.name: Straw Hat Pirates (In Crisis)
factions.description: The crew at their lowest point - ship destroyed, Robin captured, Usopp gone, and facing the World Government's elite assassins.

factions.name: Tom's Workers (Legacy)
factions.description: The legendary shipwright team that built the Oro Jackson and Sea Train. Their legacy and the Pluton blueprints drive the entire arc.

events: Straw Hats arrive seeking ship repairs
Franky Family steals 200 million berries from Usopp
Usopp beaten badly by Franky Family
Keel damage discovered - Merry cannot be repaired
Luffy decides to get a new ship
Luffy vs Usopp duel at Scrap Island
Lucci, Kaku, Kalifa, Blueno revealed as CP9
Truth revealed - Robin sacrificed herself for crew
Straw Hats and Franky Family unite
Departure for Enies Lobby on Rocket Man

quickFacts: Built entirely on water with canal streets
Tom built Gold Roger's ship here
CP9 infiltrated for 5 years as Galley-La workers
Where Going Merry received death sentence
Luffy vs Usopp duel - crew split
Robin sacrificed herself to protect crew from Buster Call
Where Thousand Sunny was later built

videos.title: Luffy vs Usopp - The Duel
videos.description: The emotionally devastating fight between captain and crewmate over the Going Merry's fate.

videos.title: Kaku jumps over water 7 buildings
videos.description: Kaku demonstrates his incredible agility by leaping over the buildings of Water 7.

videos.title: Franky Burns Pluton Blueprints
videos.description: Franky destroys the ancient weapon blueprints, showing faith in Robin.
